cross_scene_arcs.title: Enforcement of Message Discipline
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Malcolm consistently prioritizes strict control over political messaging, reacting to perceived deviations with escalating interventions—from reprimanding Simon over a linguistic error to canceling media appearances and enforcing compliance under high-stakes conditions.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Malcolm identifies Simon's misuse of 'unforseeable' as a threat to governmental credibility.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Malcolm enforces message discipline during a high-pressure rehearsal, overriding Simon's fatigue to ensure political consistency.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The tension between authentic communication and manufactured political narratives remains unaddressed, with no resolution on whether message control serves national interest or personal power.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Power Struggle with Simon Foster
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Malcolm's relationship with Simon evolves from hierarchical oversight to open antagonism, marked by repeated confrontations, public humiliation, and ultimately Simon's termination, reflecting a central conflict over authority, integrity, and loyalty.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Malcolm arrives unannounced in Simon's office to reassert dominance.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Malcolm fires Simon, cementing his authority but severing their professional relationship entirely.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The legitimacy of Malcolm's authority in dismissing Simon remains ambiguous, and the moral cost of enforcing loyalty over principle is left unexamined.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Descent into Authoritarian Control
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Malcolm transitions from a political enforcer to an authoritarian operator, using intimidation, coercion, and violence to control information and personnel, culminating in assassination orders and physical confrontations.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Malcolm reveals the existence of a secret war committee, positioning himself as gatekeeper of real power.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Malcolm resorts to public interrogation, physical intimidation, and covert operations, signaling a complete breakdown in institutional norms.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Malcolm's methods risk destroying the very mission he seeks to protect, with no accountability for his escalating abuse of power.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Fractured Loyalty and Institutional Betrayal
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Malcolm's relationship with Linton deteriorates from strained professionalism to open defiance, as he rejects directives, humiliates his superior, and asserts independent authority, reflecting a broader breakdown in hierarchical trust.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Linton informs Malcolm of his intelligence-gathering mission, creating tension over support and respect.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Malcolm publicly humiliates Linton and disengages from his authority, signaling complete insubordination.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The consequences of Malcolm's defiance—both personal and institutional—remain uncertain, with no resolution on his future within the mission structure.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Moral and Emotional Isolation
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: As Malcolm becomes increasingly isolated—emotionally, politically, and morally—his actions grow more extreme, culminating in a detached, almost ritualistic acceptance of war, while maintaining surface-level control over institutional transitions.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Malcolm is absent during a key group moment, signaling disengagement from collective purpose.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Malcolm presides over a new leadership transition with calm authority, yet remains emotionally opaque and morally unaccountable.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Malcolm's internal response to the war's onset and his own role in enabling it remains unexpressed, leaving his moral reckoning unresolved.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Erosion of Political Agency Under Party Discipline
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Simon's initial misstep in a live interview triggers a cascade of disciplinary actions from Malcolm, progressively stripping him of media access, autonomy, and political voice, culminating in his marginalization despite attempts to conform.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Simon holds a public platform and media visibility as Minister for International Development.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Simon is silenced, removed from Question Time, and placed under communication lockdown, losing control over his political narrative.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Simon remains trapped between personal conviction and institutional loyalty, unable to reclaim agency without risking total expulsion.

cross_scene_arcs.title: From Marginal Figure to Reluctant Insider
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Simon transitions from symbolic inclusion to active participation in high-level diplomacy, yet struggles to be seen as legitimate, constantly performing urgency and proximity to power to compensate for his fragile standing.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Simon is included in the Karen Clark meeting but dismissed as 'meat in the room'—present but insignificant.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Simon gains formal access to the Future Planning Committee, yet his influence remains ambiguous and performative.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: It remains unclear whether Simon's role is substantive or a political façade, leaving his value and authority in question.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Crisis of Authenticity in Political Performance
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Simon repeatedly attempts to balance authentic expression with political safety, but his efforts—whether in humor, off-script remarks, or emotional outbursts—backfire, exposing the impossibility of genuine self-expression within rigid political performance.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Simon seeks to 'shine' on Question Time by crafting a likable, humorous answer.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Simon is trapped in rehearsed messaging, unable to disengage from performance even when emotionally exhausted.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Simon continues to seek a way to be both authentic and politically viable, but no resolution is achieved.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Simon vs. Malcolm: A Fractured Power Dynamic
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: The power struggle between Simon and Malcolm evolves from professional reprimand to personal confrontation, culminating in Simon's firing, revealing a relationship defined by control, humiliation, and unresolved emotional tension.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Malcolm confronts Simon unannounced, asserting dominance over his professional conduct.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Malcolm fires Simon publicly, ending his career with threats of media retaliation, cementing total control.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Simon vows not to go quietly, but lacks the means to challenge Malcolm's authority, leaving his resistance unrealized.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Moral Dissent in the Face of War
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Simon's evolving stance on war—from accidental misstatement to moral resistance—reveals a deepening internal conflict between complicity and conscience, ultimately leading to his downfall as he refuses to fully endorse the war without evidence.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Simon's verbal error ('unforseeable') inadvertently downplays the predictability of war, sparking backlash.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Simon openly demands evidence for war, is fired, and exits in defiance, marking his transformation into a principled dissenter.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Simon's act of resistance remains isolated and ineffective in stopping the war, leaving the impact of individual conscience in a corrupt system uncertain.

cross_scene_arcs.title: From Marginal Aide to Strategic Insider
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Toby ascends from a low-ranking, socially marginalized aide to a trusted participant in high-level political strategy, gaining access to elite circles and influencing key decisions despite initial exclusion.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Toby is present but dismissed, symbolically outside the power room.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Toby secures a seat on the 'war committee,' transitioning from observer to active participant in war planning.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: His legitimacy and long-term standing within these circles remain fragile and contingent on powerful patrons.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Shifting Allegiances: Loyalty and Betrayal in the Inner Circle
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Toby evolves from a loyal Simon supporter to a figure increasingly aligned with Malcolm, reflecting a complex realignment of personal and political loyalties under pressure and ambition.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Toby seeks Simon's approval and distances himself from Judy's dissent.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Toby is claimed by Malcolm as his 'new boy,' publicly breaking from Simon and aligning with a new power center.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Toby's true loyalty remains ambiguous—he appears complicit but conflicted, lacking full acceptance from either faction.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Cost of Complicity: Moral Erosion and Ethical Crisis
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Toby becomes increasingly entangled in ethically compromised actions, culminating in direct participation in a cover-up, while his attempts to voice moral concern are rebuffed, highlighting an internal struggle between ambition and integrity.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Toby hesitates to follow Simon into unauthorized surveillance, showing concern for protocol.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Toby actively enables the deletion of critical intelligence, then confronts the delegation's moral detachment—only to be dismissed.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Toby's moral objections are isolated and ineffective, leaving him complicit without redemption or resolution.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Personal Collapse Amid Professional Ascent
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: As Toby rises professionally, his personal life unravels—losing his home, romantic connection, and emotional stability—revealing a disintegration of private identity beneath a surface of political advancement.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Toby fights to maintain his domestic life with Suzy and Michael.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Toby is emotionally rejected, professionally isolated, and physically erased from both home and office.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: No reconciliation or healing occurs; Toby exits without closure, his personal losses unacknowledged and unresolved.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Leak That Defines Him
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Toby proposes a strategic leak to protect Simon, but when it becomes public, he is scapegoated, forcing him to navigate the fallout while his role remains officially unconfirmed but personally devastating.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Toby suggests the leak as a controlled political maneuver to strengthen Simon's position.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: The leak erupts publicly, and Toby is implicated and attacked, becoming a liability rather than a strategist.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Whether Toby actually leaked the information—or was merely perceived to—remains unclear, leaving his reputation in ruins without resolution.
